name: Coconut-Chocolate Rugelach
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter and cream cheese in a large bowl with an electric mixer until smooth.
2. On low speed, beat in sugar and flour until blended.
3. Divide dough into four portions. Shape each portion into a disk. Wrap and refrigerate until firm enough to roll out, at least 4 hours.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F. Place oven rack in the upper third of the oven. Have cookie sheets ready (no need to grease).
5. Melt preserves in a microwave or small saucepan over low heat. Press through a fine strainer into a small bowl.
6. On a floured surface, roll one portion dough into a 10-inch circle. Brush with about 1/4 of the preserve. Sprinkle with 3 tablespoons shredded coconut, 2 tablespoons almonds, and 2 tablespoons chocolate chips. Add 1 tablespoon sugar.
7. Gently press filling so it adheres to dough. With a pizza wheel or knife, cut the circle into 12 wedges. Roll up from the wide edge to the point. Place 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets.
8. Repeat with remaining dough and filling.
9. Bake in the upper third of the oven for 18 to 22 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove to a wire rack to cool.
10. Dust with confectioners' sugar.
Variation: Brown Sugar-Fig Rugelach
- Replace sugar with 1/4 cup packed brown sugar.
- Filling: Replace shredded coconut with 1/2 cup finely chopped dried figs (about 4); almonds with 1/2 cup finely chopped toasted hazelnuts; chocolate chips with 1/2 cup dried Zante currants. Increase sugar to 1/2 cup and stir in 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon.
- Fill as directed, replacing plain sugar with cinnamon sugar. Cut, roll up, and bake as directed. Remove to a wire rack. While warm, brush with 2 tablespoons melted butter and sprinkle with remaining cinnamon in place of confectioners' sugar.
